shortAnswer: Rheumatoid factor is an antibody that often occurs with rheumatoid arthritis, but can also be positive with other diseases or even in healthy people. A positive value alone is not a diagnosis and must always be assessed together with symptoms and other findings.
metaDescription: Rheumatoid factor elevated? Meaning for rheumatoid arthritis explained simply.
causesElevated: Can also be mildly elevated in healthy people
doctorQuestions: Does this value confirm rheumatoid arthritis?
